rulings.Case Title: CELINA PHAM et al VS. JANET REMOLONA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-499540 WITH CASE CGC-08-471108
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 11, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFFS CELINA PHAM, JOHN KELLEY, JENNIFER MCHUGH'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-499540 WITH CASE CGC-08-471108 is granted upon the condition that moving parties stipulate to trial continuance if requested. The 2 cases have a substantial overlap of issues. Consolidation would promote judicial economy. The motion is timely considering the discovery of the individual acts and the personal issues raised in counsel's declaration. If defendants wish, they may seek a trial continuance in Department 206. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MONTGOMERY SANSOME LP VS. ZHIAN Z REZAI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 11, 2010, line 3. DEFENDANT ZHIAN REZAI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that: (1) M S Ltd. LP was the party that entered into the contracts; (2) M S Ltd. LP (through Nordeman) filed a fictitious business name statement with the San Mateo County Clerk, see Ex. F to Currie declaration; (3) the fictitious business name statement lists M S Ltd. LP as a general partnership; (4) M S Ltd. LP recorded the mechanics' lien, see Ex. G. to Currie declaration; and (5) M S Ltd. LP never had a contractor's license. Defendants are entitled to summary judgment under B & P Code section 7031. See also Opp v. St. Paul Fire & Marine Insurance Company (2007) 154 Cal App 4th 71; W S S Industrial Construction, Inc. v. Great West Contractors, Inc. (2008) 162 Cal App 4th 581. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID CONDENSA VS. MBV LAW, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration; Declaration Of Natalie P. Vance And Donald D. Bueder In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 11, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANTS MBV LAW, LLP, DON BUDER, PAUL NEIBERGS' Petition To Compel Arbitration is denied. Defendants fail to prove the existence of a valid arbitration agreement. Buder mailed a copy of the retainer agreement, which included the arbitration clause, to Helio's president, Condensa. Condensa declares he does not recall receiving a written fee/retainer agreement, did not sign one, and does not have a copy of the agreement in his files. Buden did not receive a signed copy back from Condensa. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GAEHWILER SR. VS. HECTOR MORALES et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 11, 2010, line 9. DEFENDANTS HECTOR MORALES, VIRIDIANA MILLAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 5 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead compliance with or exemption from the San Francisco Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Ordinance. See California Practice Guide Landlord-Tenant Ch. 8-B, Section 8:80 (Rutter); Wheeler v. Bainbridge (1948) 84 Cal App 2d Supp 849, and Gross v. Superior Court (1985) 171 Cal App 3d 265 that discusses rent control legislation. =(302/CWW)
